title: Views about AI's impact on society in the next 20 years
subtitle: Survey respondents were asked, "Will artificial intelligence help or harm people in the next 20 years?"
caption:
Views about AI's impact on society in the next 20 years, 2021
Survey respondents were asked, "Will artificial intelligence help or harm people in the next 20 years?"

This stacked bar chart displays the distribution of public opinion across eleven countries regarding whether artificial intelligence will mostly help, mostly harm, neither, or if respondents have no opinion. The horizontal bars represent the percentage of respondents in each category, summing to 100% for every nation surveyed. The data shows a wide divergence in outlook, with expectations of benefit ranging from a high of 61% in Japan and China down to 19% in Pakistan. In Japan and China, the blue segment representing "Mostly help" dominates the visual field, while the red segment for "Mostly harm" remains at 13%. Germany follows closely with 60% expecting help and 18% expecting harm. Moving down the list, Italy shows an even split between those expecting help (50%) and the combined groups of those expecting harm (32%) or holding no opinion (9.7%). The United Kingdom and Canada display more mixed views, with "Mostly help" dropping to 47% and 40% respectively, while the tan "No opinion or don't know" category expands to 20% and 30%. France and the United States show further polarization; in the US, the share expecting help (35%) equals the share expecting harm (35%), with 29% unsure. India mirrors this uncertainty with 33% expecting harm and 30% unsure. Indonesia presents a distinct pattern where the largest single group (44%) holds no opinion, while only 22% expect help. Pakistan records the lowest optimism, with just 19% expecting AI to mostly help, compared to 52% who believe it will mostly harm. The survey included a global total of 120,000–130,000 people aged 15+, with most countries utilizing a nationally representative sample of around 1,000 people. The visible pattern indicates that optimism about AI's future impact is highest in East Asia and lowest in South Asia, where skepticism and uncertainty prevail.
